title: Trials; wisdom; hearing and doing
summary_text: Greetings—count trials joy knowing testing produces endurance; lacking wisdom ask God generously without doubting—double-minded unstable. Lowly brother glory high; rich humiliation. Temptation not from God—lust conceives sin; Father lights no variation. Be doers of word not hearers only.
key_points: Count it all joy when you fall into various temptations.
If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God.
But let him ask in faith, without any doubting.
Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above.
But be doers of the word, and not only hearers, deluding your own selves.
evidence_phrases: Count it all joy
If any of you lacks wisdom
ask in faith, without any doubting
Every good gift … is from above
of his own will he gave birth to us
be doers of the word

title: No partiality; faith without works dead
summary_text: Faith in Lord glory—do not hold faith with partiality—gold ring vs poor—judges with evil thoughts. Royal law love neighbor—mercy triumphs. Faith without works dead—demons believe and shudder—Abraham justified by works offering Isaac; Rahab justified.
key_points: My brothers, don’t hold the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ of glory with partiality.
You shall love your neighbor as yourself.
Mercy triumphs over judgment.
Faith, if it has no works, is dead in itself.
You see that faith worked with his works, and by works faith was perfected.
evidence_phrases: don’t hold the faith … with partiality
You shall love your neighbor as yourself
Mercy triumphs over judgment
faith, if it has no works, is dead
Abraham … offered up Isaac
You see that faith worked with his works
Rahab … was justified by works

title: Taming tongue; wisdom from above
summary_text: Not many teachers—greater condemnation. Stumble in word—bridle whole body; tongue small rudder great ship; fire world iniquity; no tame beast tames tongue—blessing cursing—fresh bitter not. Wisdom from above pure peaceable gentle full mercy good fruits.
key_points: Not many of you should be teachers, my brothers, knowing that we will receive heavier judgment.
Indeed, we all stumble in many things. If anyone doesn’t stumble in word, the same is a perfect person.
The tongue is also a little member, and boasts great things.
With it we bless our Lord and Father, and with it we curse men.
The wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceful, gentle, reasonable.
evidence_phrases: Not many of you should be teachers
we all stumble in many things
the tongue is a fire
With it we bless … and … we curse
does a spring send out … fresh and bitter?
The wisdom that is from above

title: Wars from lust; tomorrow uncertain; resist devil
summary_text: Wars fights from pleasures lusts; friendship world enmity God—greater grace. Resist devil flee draw near God cleanse hands purify hearts humble. Do not speak evil judge lawgiver—who judge neighbor. Boast not tomorrow—vapor. If Lord wills. Sin to know good not do.
key_points: Where do wars and fightings among you come from? Aren’t they from your pleasures that war in your members?
friendship with the world is enmity with God.
Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you.
There is only one lawgiver, who is able to save and to destroy.
For you ought to say, ‘If the Lord wills, we will both live, and do this or that.’
evidence_phrases: your pleasures that war in your members
friendship with the world is enmity with God
Draw near to God
Resist the devil
There is only one lawgiver
If the Lord wills

title: Rich weep; patience; prayer; restore wanderer
summary_text: Rich weep miseries coming—rusted riches witness against you. Be patient till Lord’s coming—farmer waits. Grumble not—Judge stands door. Prophets suffering patience Job end Lord compassionate merciful. Swear not—pray sing sick call elders anoint—confess—righteous effective fervent. Wanderer bring back save soul death.
key_points: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries that are coming on you.
Be patient therefore, brothers, until the coming of the Lord.
The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective.
if anyone among you wanders from the truth, and someone turns him back, let him know that he who turns a sinner from the error of his way will save a soul from death.
evidence_phrases: you rich, weep and howl
Be patient … until the coming of the Lord
The prayer of a righteous person is powerful
Elijah … prayed earnestly
if anyone among you wanders from the truth
will save a soul from death
